issues.description: References .NET 6 SDK for nullable reference types example. While the feature works in .NET 9, referencing an older SDK version may confuse learners about current requirements.
issues.webResearchNote: Nullable reference types were introduced in C# 8 (.NET Core 3.0) and are fully supported in .NET 9. The .NET 6 reference is not incorrect but could be updated to .NET 9 for consistency.

issues.description: Correctly identifies C# 8 as the introduction point for nullable reference types feature.
issues.webResearchNote: Verified: Nullable reference types introduced in C# 8.0 - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/csharp/nullable-references

## Breaking Down the Syntax

**`while (condition)`**: The loop checks the condition BEFORE each iteration. If true, the loop body runs. If fals
issues.description: Theory file is truncated/incomplete (142 words). The content appears to be cut off mid-sentence at 'If fals'.
References .NET 8 for collection expressions. While C# 12 collection expressions work in .NET 9, the warning about .NET 8 vs .NET 7 compatibility is outdated.
issues.webResearchNote: Collection expressions were introduced in C# 12 (.NET 8). They work in .NET 9. The content should mention .NET 9 as the current version while noting backward compatibility.

issues.description: References .NET 7 as unsupported for collection expressions. While accurate, this is outdated pedagogy for a .NET 9 course.
issues.webResearchNote: For a .NET 9 course, focus should be on current .NET 9/C# 13 features rather than warning about .NET 7 limitations.

issues.description: Correctly identifies C# 11 for required modifier feature.
issues.webResearchNote: Verified: The 'required' modifier was introduced in C# 11.0 - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/csharp/language-reference/keywords/required

issues.description: Correctly identifies C# 6 for expression-bodied members feature.
issues.webResearchNote: Verified: Expression-bodied members introduced in C# 6.0 - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/csharp/programming-guide/statements-expressions-operators/expression-bodied-members

issues.description: References .NET 6 SDK for string interpolation examples. Could be updated to .NET 9 for consistency with target version.
issues.webResearchNote: String interpolation has been supported since C# 6.0 and works in all newer versions including .NET 9.
